message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_nonzero of 0-d scalar reflects value (frankentorch-88p)

tensor_nonzero used `indices.len().checked_div(ndim).unwrap_or(0)` to
recover the non-zero count from the flattened indices buffer, which
collapses to 0/0 -> 0 whenever the input is a 0-d scalar. As a result
torch.nonzero(torch.tensor(5.0)) and torch.nonzero(torch.tensor(0.0))
both produced shape [0, 0], silently losing the "is non-zero" signal
on scalars.

Track `num_nonzero` explicitly instead. PyTorch parity now holds for
all rank-0 cases: non-zero -> [1, 0], zero -> [0, 0], NaN -> [1, 0]
(NaN is treated as non-zero, matching PyTorch and existing rank>=1
tests).

Tests:
- nonzero_scalar_nonzero_returns_shape_1_by_0
- nonzero_scalar_zero_returns_shape_0_by_0
- nonzero_scalar_nan_is_nonzero
All 10 nonzero tests pass; full ft-api suite (1082 tests) green;
clippy + ubs clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
